title: Critic-R: Improving Agentic Search using Instruction-tuned Retrievers with Natural Language Introspective Feedback
desc: Proposes Critic-R, a framework that improves agentic search through retriever optimization. Combines Critic-R-Zero, an inference-time loop that iteratively rewrites queries using introspective feedback, with Critic-Embed, which trains retrievers from automatically supervised refinement trajectories. Improves retrieval quality and answer accuracy on HotpotQA, 2WikiMultihopQA, MuSiQue, and Bamboogle.

title: GrepSeek: Training Search Agents for Direct Corpus Interaction
desc: Introduces GrepSeek, where LLM agents search corpora by executing shell commands rather than relying on keyword-based retrieval. Uses a two-stage training approach (verified trajectory generation followed by policy optimization) and a parallel execution engine for fast shell-based retrieval, showing strong results across seven QA benchmarks.

title: Evaluation of Agents under Simulated AI Marketplace Dynamics
desc: Introduces a simulation-based evaluation framework for information access systems as participants in competitive AI marketplaces. Measures marketplace-level outcomes like retention and market share, capturing early-adoption advantages and competitive dynamics overlooked by static benchmarks.

title: Learning to Reason for Multi-Step Retrieval of Personal Context in Personalized Question Answering
desc: Introduces PR2, a reinforcement learning framework that trains LLMs to decide when and what to retrieve from user profiles across multiple reasoning steps for personalized QA. Achieves 8.8–12% relative improvement over existing approaches on LaMP-QA.

title: Improving User Privacy in Personalized Generation: Client-Side Retrieval-Augmented Modification of Server-Side Generated Speculations
desc: Proposes P³, a system where a large server-side model generates draft tokens from the query alone while a small client-side model refines them using private user profiles. Recovers 90–96% of full-personalization quality while limiting private data exposure.

title: Beyond a Million Tokens: Benchmarking and Enhancing Long-Term Memory in LLMs
desc: Introduces BEAM, a benchmark with conversations up to 10M tokens, and LIGHT, a cognitive-inspired framework equipping LLMs with episodic, working, and scratchpad memory. Shows even 1M-context models struggle with long dialogues, while LIGHT improves recall by 3.5–12.7%.

title: Pathways of Thoughts: Multi-Directional Thinking for Long-form Personalized Question Answering
desc: Proposes PoT, an inference-time technique that generates multiple reasoning trajectories by dynamically selecting cognitive operations (reason, revise, personalize, clarify), then combines candidate responses weighted by inferred user preferences. Achieves up to 10.8% relative improvement on LaMP-QA.

title: LLM-based Multi-Agent Blackboard System for Information Discovery in Data Science
desc: Introduces a blackboard-inspired multi-agent architecture where a central agent broadcasts tasks and specialized agents volunteer based on their expertise, eliminating rigid hierarchical coordination. Achieves 13–57% relative improvements on data science benchmarks over the best baseline.

title: Learning from Natural Language Feedback for Personalized Question Answering
desc: Proposes VAC, which replaces scalar reward signals with natural language feedback derived from user profiles to train personalized response models via iterative self-training. The final model requires no feedback at inference time and achieves state-of-the-art results on LaMP-QA.

title: The Second Tutorial on Retrieval-Enhanced Machine Learning: Synthesis and Opportunities
desc: Tutorial synthesizing retrieval-enhanced machine learning paradigms across NLP, vision, and computational biology, presenting a unified formal framework that bridges foundational IR research with modern RAG systems.

title: CIIR@LiveRAG 2025: Optimizing Multi-Agent Retrieval Augmented Generation through Self-Training
desc: Presents mRAG, a multi-agent RAG system with specialized agents for planning, searching, and reasoning, trained via reward-guided self-training over sampled trajectories. Demonstrated strong performance in the SIGIR 2025 LiveRAG competition.

title: LaMP-QA: A Benchmark for Personalized Long-form Question Answering
desc: Introduces LaMP-QA, a benchmark for personalized long-form QA spanning 45+ subcategories across three broad domains. Shows that systems incorporating personalized context achieve up to 39% performance improvement over non-personalized baselines.

title: Plan-and-Refine: Diverse and Comprehensive Retrieval-Augmented Generation
desc: Proposes a two-phase RAG framework that first generates a diverse set of query-aspect plans, then refines candidate responses using those plans and selects the best output via a reward model. Achieves up to 15.4% improvement on TREC datasets.

title: Personalized Generation In Large Model Era: A Survey
desc: The first comprehensive survey on personalized generation with large models, establishing a unified framework and multi-level taxonomy covering technical advances, datasets, and evaluation across modalities and tasks. Discusses open challenges and future directions.

title: ExPerT: Effective and Explainable Evaluation of Personalized Long-Form Text Generation
desc: Introduces ExPerT, an LLM-based evaluation framework that extracts atomic aspects and evidence from generated and reference texts, then measures alignment on content and writing style with detailed explanations. Outperforms existing metrics by 7.2% in alignment with human judgments.

title: Reasoning-Enhanced Self-Training for Long-Form Personalized Text Generation
desc: Introduces REST-PG, training LLMs to reason through personal data before generating long-form responses via an EM-based reinforced self-training loop that progressively refines on the model's own high-quality outputs. Achieves 14.5% average relative improvement on LongLaMP.

title: Beyond Factual Accuracy: Evaluating Coverage of Diverse Factual Information in Long-form Text Generation
desc: Introduces ICAT, a modular evaluation framework that decomposes LLM-generated text into atomic claims, validates them against retrieved knowledge sources, and measures coverage of expected factual aspects. Shows strong agreement with human evaluations on TREC Web Track data.

title: Accelerating Retrieval-Augmented Generation
desc: Finds that exact nearest-neighbor retrieval, despite higher cost, can reduce overall RAG inference time by improving document quality. Introduces IKS, a near-memory hardware accelerator achieving 13–28× faster exact retrieval on 512GB databases.

title: Learning to Rank for Multiple Retrieval-Augmented Models through Iterative Utility Maximization
desc: Proposes a unified search engine for serving multiple heterogeneous RAG models, using iterative expectation-maximization to maximize each agent's utility based on offline document quality feedback. Significantly outperforms baselines across 18 RAG models on KILT.

title: Comparing Retrieval-Augmentation and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Privacy-Preserving Personalization of Large Language Models
desc: First systematic comparison of RAG vs. PEFT as privacy-preserving personalization strategies across seven LaMP tasks. Finds RAG excels for data-scarce users (+14.9%) while PEFT wins with abundant user data, and their combination reaches +16.0%.

title: Tutorial on Retrieval-Enhanced Machine Learning: Synthesis and Opportunities
desc: Tutorial at SIGIR-AP 2024 presenting a formal unified framework for retrieval-enhanced machine learning, extending RAG paradigms beyond NLP to computer vision, time series prediction, and computational biology.

title: Retrieval-Enhanced Machine Learning: Synthesis and Opportunities
desc: Introduces a formal framework for retrieval-enhanced machine learning with consistent notation across ML domains, bridging foundational IR research with contemporary REML literature across NLP, vision, and computational biology.

title: LongLaMP: A Benchmark for Personalized Long-form Text Generation
desc: Introduces LongLaMP, the first benchmark focused on personalized long-form text generation, covering tasks such as email composition and review writing. Demonstrates that personalization consistently benefits long-form generation across all tasks.

title: LaMP: When Large Language Models Meet Personalization
desc: Introduces LaMP, a benchmark of seven personalization tasks (three classification, four generation), and proposes retrieval-augmented methods using term-based, semantic, and time-aware strategies to personalize LLM outputs from user profiles.

title: Towards a Search Engine for Machines: Unified Ranking for Multiple Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models
desc: Introduces uRAG, a unified retrieval engine that serves 36 diverse downstream RAG systems spanning QA, fact verification, entity linking, and relation extraction. Establishes training guidelines for machine-oriented search engines optimized for downstream task utility.

title: Evaluating Retrieval Quality in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
desc: Proposes eRAG, which evaluates each retrieved document individually by measuring its contribution to downstream task performance, achieving Kendall's τ improvements of 0.168–0.494 over traditional methods at up to 50× lower GPU cost.

title: Optimization Methods for Personalizing Large Language Models through Retrieval Augmentation
desc: First work to optimize retrieval models that deliver personal documents to LLMs for customized output, developing RL-based and knowledge distillation approaches. Achieves statistically significant improvements on 6 of 7 LaMP benchmark tasks.

title: Pre-Training Multi-Modal Dense Retrievers for Outside-Knowledge Visual Question Answering
desc: Proposes an automatic pipeline for generating pre-training data for outside-knowledge VQA retrieval models. Achieves 26.9% Precision@5 improvement over asymmetric architectures and strong zero-shot generalization with less training data.

title: A Symmetric Dual Encoding Dense Retrieval Framework for Knowledge-Intensive Visual Question Answering
desc: Introduces DEDR, a dense retrieval framework for knowledge-intensive VQA that encodes queries and documents into a shared space via uni-modal and multi-modal encoders with iterative distillation. Improves retrieval by 11.6–30.9% and enables state-of-the-art end-to-end VQA via MM-FiD.

title: PEACH: Pre-Training Sequence-to-Sequence Multilingual Models for Translation with Semi-Supervised Pseudo-Parallel Document Generation
desc: Proposes semi-supervised pseudo-parallel document generation for pre-training multilingual seq2seq models in low-resource translation settings, reducing dependence on large parallel corpora.

title: ARMAN: Pre-training with Semantically Selecting and Reordering of Sentences for Persian Abstractive Summarization
desc: Introduces ARMAN, a Transformer encoder-decoder pre-trained with objectives that select semantically salient sentences and reorder them to match human writing conventions. Achieves state-of-the-art results across six Persian summarization benchmarks.

title: UTNLP at SemEval-2021 Task 5: A Comparative Analysis of Toxic Span Detection using Attention-based, Named Entity Recognition, and Ensemble Models
desc: Comparative analysis of attention-based, NER-based, and ensemble approaches for toxic span detection at SemEval-2021, evaluating strengths and trade-offs of each paradigm on the shared task dataset.
